evidence_excerpt: Older sibling (patient 1) developed hypergonadotropic hypogonadism and gynecomastia in adolescence but ‘responded well to treatment’: gynecomastia and elevated gonadotropins/estradiol regressed with testosterone supplementation; ongoing endocrine follow-up and hormone replacement indicate residual issues.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Regular endocrinological control and continued testosterone supplementation for hypergonadotropic hypogonadism; gynecomastia and hormone abnormalities regressed, but treatment requirement persists.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: After entering adolescence, patient 1 developed gynecomastia and labs from age 14 showed hypergonadotropic hypogonadism with low testosterone.
The older brother receives testosterone supplementation for completing his puberty (initiation of replacement therapy).
He responded well to treatment: gynecomastia and elevated gonadotropic hormone and estradiol levels regressed owing to negative feedback.

notes_on_uncertainty: This case report includes two siblings; long-term follow-up details are primarily available for patient 1 (older brother). The reference time approximates the point when ovotesticular tissue was histologically confirmed and management plans (surgical correction, endocrine follow-up) were formulated. Functional status is inferred conservatively from treatment response and lack of reported activity limitations.
